items.title: Diphtheria response criticised for not being fast enough

children.content: Rapid vaccination could stem the spread of the potentially deadly diphtheria outbreak, but the government's response has been criticised as being too slow.

articles.title: NT health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across the outback

children.content: As diphtheria spreads across four different Australian jurisdictions in the largest outbreak since records began, public health messaging in the NT — the region most impacted by the serious disease — has been hard to come by.

image.altText: A woman holding up her t-shirt sleeve, revealing a small band-aid over the spot where she was vaccinated.

articles.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise

children.content: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

image.altText: A doctor with his arms folded stands in front of a window

articles.title: Outbreak of highly contagious infection grows to more than 220 cases

children.content: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

gallery.altText: Dr Casey smiles into the camera.

thumbnail.altText: Dr Casey smiles into the camera.

items.title: Man on the run after reportedly raping elderly woman in her own home

children.content: A man who police say raped an elderly woman in her own home on Wednesday night before fleeing the scene remains on the run while investigators appeal for information.

image.altText: The bonnent of an NT police car that reads "police" and has the NT police force emblem. Green leaves blurred in right corner.

items.title: From two-way radio to satellite, School of the Air celebrates 75 years

children.content: The world's oldest school of the air is celebrating its 75th anniversary in Alice Springs. It provides a service to families over 1.3 million square kilometres and also is known as the world's largest classroom.

image.altText: A young girl speaking into an old radio in the 1950s.

items.title: NT Chief Minister dismisses concerns about child protection changes as 'hysteria'

children.content: Northern Territory Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro has labelled concerns about proposed child protection changes as "hysteria", a day after the prime minister called for "respectful" consultation with outraged Aboriginal groups.

image.altText: A side profile of a woman facing a microphone

items.title: Former NT political staffer claims unfair dismissal, airs bullying allegations

children.content: A former staffer for Northern Territory government backbencher Clinton Howe is claiming she was sacked for refusing to falsify constituent information in a database, and was bullied and micromanaged by the politician's full-time electorate officer.

image.altText: A large photo of a smiling politician fills the front window of an office building, the front door reads: Clinton Howe

items.title: Parents of children in foster care say 'black eyes' and bite marks were ignored

children.content: The parents of two children in Northern Territory foster care have spoken out about their "heartbreaking" experience with the system, saying concerns their daughter was being abused by her foster family were brushed off by the department.

gallery.altText: A woman in a sleeveless, long dress, long brown hair in kitchen, blurred background, with brown teddy bear focus in foreground.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a sleeveless, long dress, long brown hair in kitchen, blurred background, with brown teddy bear focus in foreground.

items.title: Nampijinpa Price: 'Extremist ideology' destroying Australian culture

children.content: Jacinta Nampijinpa Price has denied that she agreed with a podcaster saying Australia should stop 'flooding' Australia with migrants from Asia, Africa and the Middle East.

image.altText: Three people walking - men either side of one Aboriginal woman in focus. She has a soft expression, dark blue top and blazer on.

items.title: Defence minister 'disappointed' by lawsuit over Darwin port ownership

children.content: Chinese-owned company Landbridge launched legal action earlier this year following a pledge by the Prime Minister to return the port to Australian hands. Defence Minister Richard Marles says the government will fight the suit and remained committed to finding a domestic buyer.

image.altText: White man standing in front of podium, light pink shirt, navy blue pants, mid-speech, man behind him in white shirt.

items.title: Arafura Rare Earths mine gets green light to challenge China's dominance

children.content: Arafura Rare Earths has announced its flagship project north of Alice Springs will go ahead, decades after it was first flagged as a prospect.

image.altText: a drone shot of a mining camp in Central Australia.

items.title: Peak body slams ministers' failure to attend Aboriginal housing talks

children.content: The Northern Territory's peak body for Aboriginal housing says it's "disgraceful" ministers from both levels of government have failed to attend a conference on the housing crisis.

image.altText: A woman with curly brown hair and in a brown collared shirt looks at the camera

items.title: PM leaves door open to discretionary trust tax changes

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has left the door open to tweaking a proposed change to how a discretionary trust is taxed.  Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese looking down in a suit while touching his ear.

items.title: Farm worker found not guilty of circular saw amputation murder

children.content: The jury has returned its verdict in the case of a man who killed pensioner Kalman Tal. The 66-year-old died shortly after paying $5,000 to have his leg amputated in a park.

image.altText: John Yalu banana farm

items.title: Cuba on the brink: Residents describe life 'trapped between two psychopaths'

children.content: As power and petrol supplies run out, Cubans tell the ABC about their daily lives. "You can no longer find streets packed with travellers, music and dance," one says. "Only Cuban nationals, still attempting to take some bread home."

articles.title: Cuba accepts offer of $140m in American aid, Rubio says

children.content: Tensions between the longtime Cold War rivals have hit a new low in recent days after the US indicted the country's 94-year-old former president Raúl Castro.

image.altText: Marco Rubio speaking to reporters outside.

items.title: Guzman Y Gomez soars on US exit, ASX climbs higher

children.content: Australian markets have risen this morning, with Guzman Y Gomez surging on news it has pulled out of the US market. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A close up of a yellow circle with black outlines of two faces with black writing that says "Guzman y Gomez Mexican Kitchen".

items.title: Snowy bosses paid $1.2m in bonuses as hydro project costs spiral

children.content: The renewables project's chief executive, Dennis Barnes, received $323,000 in bonus pay on top of his $1.69 million salary last financial year, while four other senior executives received about $919,000 in "variable pay".

image.altText: A man wearing a construction outfit, speaking at a lectern.

items.title: Written off as 'parasites', young Indians back a cockroach in politics

children.content: A satirical political party in India amasses the support of millions in just a few days and politicians are taking notice.

gallery.altText: An AI-generated image of a cockroach at a lectern.

thumbnail.altText: An AI-generated image of a cockroach at a lectern.

items.title: Media shut out of court during bid to block release of corruption report

children.content: A secret party is trying to block the release of a report from Victoria's corruption watchdog, which examined allegations involving the state government and firefighters.

image.altText: A photo showing Ibac Independent Broad-Based Anti-Corruption Commission Victoria on the windows of its Victorian office.

items.title: US prison officials call off prisoner's execution after 'botched' attempt

children.content: Corrections officials in Tennessee halt an inmate's execution by lethal injection after medical staff repeatedly fail to find a vein.

image.altText: A mug shot of a man with a moustache and goatee

items.title: Do robotic lawnmowers actually work and are they worth the investment?

children.content: Experts say robot lawnmowers are becoming more accessible, but there are some key features to look out for.

gallery.altText: A generic image of a robot lawnmower cutting grass

thumbnail.altText: A generic image of a robot lawnmower cutting grass

items.title: The hidden codes and Aussie influence in Harry Connick Jr's new symphony

children.content: Harry Connick Jr's new symphony, Elaboratio, is filled with musical tributes to his mother Anita. Orchestra Victoria and Australian conductor Jessica Gethin are bringing those memories to life at Carnegie Hall.

image.altText: A man in a black jacket with an earpiece holds a microphone with a red and purple background

items.title: Friday news quiz: A pumped prince, a grinning sculpture and a stowaway frog

children.content: You'll be as happy as Prince William when you take this week's quiz, which goes off like a frog in a
bag of lettuce.

image.altText: Kylie Minogue sings into a microphone while wearing a rainbow-coloured dress.

items.title: 'Is that a UFO?' Suspected meteor lights up skies across NSW

children.content: Colourful streaks across the skies have been spotted from Bondi to Canberra as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere overnight.

image.altText: A golfer at a driving range lines up a shot as a meteor lights up the sky.

gallery.altText: Dashcam footage showing a flashing appearing across the sky

thumbnail.altText: Dashcam footage showing a flashing appearing across the sky

items.title: Inside the industrial-scale slave system run by Islamic State

children.content: Australia is the latest country to prosecute its own citizens over Islamic State's system of Yazidi slavery, part of a growing global reckoning over the role some foreign women allegedly played in the atrocities.

image.altText: Two veiled women walk past a billboard that urges women to wear a hijab

items.title: Republican revolt over Trump 'weaponisation' fund stalls ICE funding vote

children.content: US Senate Republicans have furious internal disagreements over a proposed compensation fund for Trump allies who claim they have been unfairly targeted by federal agencies.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaking in the aisle on board Air Force One.

items.title: There are 40,000 trusts linked to Australian farms. Here's why

children.content: Despite winning a carve-out to changes to family trusts announced in the budget, farmers are still worried about what tax reform means for them.

items.title: Global mental health disorder cases double in three decades

children.content: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.

image.altText: illustration of a woman's face cut into slides on an orange background

items.title: Aerial shooting to resume as brumby numbers spike in national park

children.content: Wild horse numbers in the NSW Snowy Mountains are back up with a reproductive control trial now being considered as aerial shooting has been confirmed to resume.

image.altText: A brown brumby with a blond mane stands in the sun in Kosciuszko National Park.

items.title: Another four platypuses re-homed into Royal National Park

children.content: There are at least 20 platypuses now living in the NSW Royal National Park's waterways, three years after their historic reintroduction.

image.altText: Unidentified person releases a platypus from a bag at night at a river bank

items.title: 36ers eye dream team as former Perth coach reunites with Bryce Cotton

children.content: One of Australian basketball's most successful coaches and one of the national league's greatest players have paired in Adelaide.

image.altText: A composite image of two men

items.title: Man denied bail after allegedly building explosives in rural home

children.content: A Bathurst man has been charged with explosives and weapons offences as specialist police teams continue to search his home in a quiet regional street.

image.altText: A Hazardous Materials Unit and fire truck parked on a street, with a red HAZMAT tent set up on grass.

items.title: Why Kotoni Staggs is making his Origin battles personal

children.content: When Kotoni Staggs takes the field he transforms into the angriest man in the NRL and upon his return to the New South Wales side the Brisbane centre is ready to channel his rage into State of Origin.

image.altText: GettyImages-2276826260

items.title: Man charged with making threats to kill on Melbourne's Bourke Street

children.content: Police said there was no broader threat to the community on Thursday night, despite the City of Melbourne sending a text message to its staff advising them to avoid the area.

image.altText: A police car with its lights on.

items.title: DNA study finds many dingoes may have some domestic dog genetics

children.content: A new study into dingo DNA has found many animals previously considered to be purebred actually have domestic dog genetics.

gallery.altText: Dingo on the beach at Fraser Island.

thumbnail.altText: Dingo on the beach at Fraser Island.

items.title: Doctors push for e-bike ban in SA for riders aged under 16

children.content: South Australia's peak medical body is calling for reforms to current e-bike laws in the state, including a ban on children aged under 16 from riding them.

image.altText: E bikes lined up in a row inside a bike shop

items.title: Chalmers asked to explain how he 'landed on 30 per cent' tax rate

children.content: The Productivity Commission was not asked to model how changes to capital gains tax would impact Australia's economy. Business groups say Treasurer Jim Chalmers must explain how the government concluded its tax changes would ultimately be a boon to productivity.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers speaks to reporters.
